channel.title: STYLAN Blog
channel.description: Thoughts, tutorials and insights about web development

item.title: React Hooks е…Ҙй—ЁжҢҮеҚ—
item.content:encoded:
д»Җд№ҲжҳҜ React Hooksпјҹ

React Hooks жҳҜ React 16.8 еј•е…Ҙзҡ„ж–°зү№жҖ§пјҢе®ғе…Ғи®ёдҪ еңЁдёҚзј–еҶҷ class зҡ„жғ…еҶөдёӢдҪҝз”Ё state д»ҘеҸҠе…¶д»–зҡ„ React зү№жҖ§гҖӮ

дёәд»Җд№ҲдҪҝз”Ё Hooksпјҹ

еңЁ Hooks еҮәзҺ°д№ӢеүҚпјҢеҰӮжһңжҲ‘д»¬жғіеңЁз»„д»¶дёӯдҪҝз”Ё stateпјҢе°ұеҝ…йЎ»дҪҝз”Ё class з»„д»¶гҖӮиҝҷеёҰжқҘдәҶдёҖдәӣй—®йўҳпјҡ 1. еӨҚжқӮзҡ„з»„д»¶йҖ»иҫ‘пјҡйҡҸзқҖз»„д»¶еҠҹиғҪзҡ„еўһеҠ пјҢз”ҹе‘Ҫе‘Ёжңҹж–№жі•дёӯзҡ„йҖ»иҫ‘дјҡеҸҳеҫ—и¶ҠжқҘи¶ҠеӨҚжқӮ 2. йҡҫд»ҘеӨҚз”ЁзҠ¶жҖҒйҖ»иҫ‘пјҡеңЁз»„д»¶д№Ӣй—ҙеӨҚз”ЁзҠ¶жҖҒйҖ»иҫ‘еҸҳеҫ—еӣ°йҡҫ 3. йҡҫд»ҘзҗҶи§Јзҡ„ classпјҡthis з»‘е®ҡгҖҒз”ҹе‘Ҫе‘Ёжңҹж–№жі•зӯүжҰӮеҝөеҜ№ж–°жүӢдёҚеҸӢеҘҪ

еёёз”Ё Hooks

useState

useState жҳҜжңҖеҹәзЎҖзҡ„ HookпјҢз”ЁдәҺеңЁеҮҪж•°з»„д»¶дёӯж·»еҠ  stateпјҡ 

import { useState } from "react";

function Counter() {
  const [count, setCount] = useState(0);

  return (
    <div>
      <p>You clicked {count} times</p>
      <button onClick={() => setCount(count + 1)}>Click me</button>
    </div>
  );
}

useEffect

useEffect з”ЁдәҺеӨ„зҗҶеүҜдҪңз”ЁпјҢеҰӮж•°жҚ®иҺ·еҸ–гҖҒи®ўйҳ…жҲ–жүӢеҠЁдҝ®ж”№ DOMпјҡ 

import { useState, useEffect } from "react";

function Example() {
  const [count, setCount] = useState(0);

  useEffect(() => {
    document.title = You clicked ${count} times;
  });

  return (
    <div>
      <p>You clicked {count} times</p>
      <button onClick={() => setCount(count + 1)}>Click me</button>
    </div>
  );
}

useContext

useContext з”ЁдәҺеңЁз»„д»¶ж ‘дёӯдј йҖ’ж•°жҚ®пјҢйҒҝе…ҚйҖҗеұӮдј йҖ’ propsпјҡ 

const ThemeContext = React.createContext("light");

function ThemedButton() {
  const theme = useContext(ThemeContext);
  return <button className={theme}>I am styled by theme context!</button>;
}

иҮӘе®ҡд№ү Hooks

иҮӘе®ҡд№ү Hooks жҳҜеӨҚз”ЁзҠ¶жҖҒйҖ»иҫ‘зҡ„дёҖз§Қж–№ејҸгҖӮйҖҡиҝҮиҮӘе®ҡд№ү HookпјҢдҪ еҸҜд»Ҙе°Ҷз»„д»¶йҖ»иҫ‘жҸҗеҸ–еҲ°еҸҜйҮҚз”Ёзҡ„еҮҪж•°дёӯгҖӮ 

function useWindowSize() {
  const [size, setSize] = useState({
    width: window.innerWidth,
    height: window.innerHeight,
  });

  useEffect(() => {
    const handleResize = () => {
      setSize({
        width: window.innerWidth,
        height: window.innerHeight,
      });
    };

    window.addEventListener("resize", handleResize);
    return () => window.removeEventListener("resize", handleResize);
  }, []);

  return size;
}

жҖ»з»“

React Hooks жҸҗдҫӣдәҶдёҖз§Қжӣҙз®ҖжҙҒгҖҒжӣҙзӣҙи§Ӯзҡ„ж–№ејҸжқҘзј–еҶҷ React з»„д»¶гҖӮйҖҡиҝҮдҪҝз”Ё HooksпјҢжҲ‘д»¬еҸҜд»Ҙпјҡ
  • еңЁеҮҪж•°з»„д»¶дёӯдҪҝз”Ё state е’Ңе…¶д»– React зү№жҖ§
  • жӣҙе®№жҳ“ең°еӨҚз”ЁзҠ¶жҖҒйҖ»иҫ‘
  • и®©д»Јз Ғжӣҙжҳ“дәҺзҗҶи§Је’ҢжөӢиҜ•
еёҢжңӣиҝҷзҜҮж–Үз« иғҪеё®еҠ©дҪ е…Ҙй—Ё React HooksпјҒ !еЁңеЁңиҺү
item.author: duan-deqing@foxmail.com (STYLAN)

item.title: Getting Started with React Hooks
item.description: Learn how to use React Hooks to build more elegant and maintainable components.
item.content:encoded:
What are React Hooks?

React Hooks is a new feature introduced in React 16.8 that allows you to use state and other React features without writing a class.

Why use Hooks?

Before Hooks appeared, if we wanted to use state in a component, we had to use class components. This brought some problems: 1. Complex component logic: As component functionality increases, the logic in lifecycle methods becomes more and more complex 2. Difficult to reuse state logic: It becomes difficult to reuse state logic between components 3. Difficult to understand classes: Concepts like this binding and lifecycle methods are not friendly to beginners

Common Hooks

useState

useState is the most basic Hook, used to add state to function components: 

import { useState } from "react";

function Counter() {
  const [count, setCount] = useState(0);

  return (
    <div>
      <p>You clicked {count} times</p>
      <button onClick={() => setCount(count + 1)}>Click me</button>
    </div>
  );
}

useEffect

useEffect is used to handle side effects, such as data fetching, subscriptions, or manual DOM manipulation: 

import { useState, useEffect } from "react";

function Example() {
  const [count, setCount] = useState(0);

  useEffect(() => {
    document.title = You clicked ${count} times;
  });

  return (
    <div>
      <p>You clicked {count} times</p>
      <button onClick={() => setCount(count + 1)}>Click me</button>
    </div>
  );
}

useContext

useContext is used to pass data through the component tree without passing props down manually at every level: 

const ThemeContext = React.createContext("light");

function ThemedButton() {
  const theme = useContext(ThemeContext);
  return <button className={theme}>I am styled by theme context!</button>;
}

Custom Hooks

Custom Hooks are a way to reuse state logic. Through custom Hooks, you can extract component logic into reusable functions. 

function useWindowSize() {
  const [size, setSize] = useState({
    width: window.innerWidth,
    height: window.innerHeight,
  });

  useEffect(() => {
    const handleResize = () => {
      setSize({
        width: window.innerWidth,
        height: window.innerHeight,
      });
    };

    window.addEventListener("resize", handleResize);
    return () => window.removeEventListener("resize", handleResize);
  }, []);

  return size;
}

Summary

React Hooks provides a more concise and intuitive way to write React components. By using Hooks, we can:
  • Use state and other React features in function components
  • Reuse state logic more easily
  • Make code easier to understand and test
Hopefully this article helps you get started with React Hooks! !еЁңеЁңиҺү
item.author: duan-deqing@foxmail.com (STYLAN)

item.title: жһ„е»әзҺ°д»ЈдёӘдәәдё»йЎө
item.content:encoded:
дёәд»Җд№ҲйңҖиҰҒдёӘдәәдё»йЎөпјҹ

дҪңдёәдёҖеҗҚејҖеҸ‘иҖ…пјҢжӢҘжңүдёҖдёӘдёӘдәәдё»йЎөжҳҜеұ•зӨәдҪ жҠҖиғҪе’ҢйЎ№зӣ®зҡ„жңҖдҪіж–№ејҸгҖӮе®ғеҸҜд»Ҙеё®еҠ©дҪ пјҡ 1. еұ•зӨәдҪ зҡ„дҪңе“Ғпјҡи®©жҪңеңЁйӣҮдё»жҲ–е®ўжҲ·зңӢеҲ°дҪ зҡ„е®һйҷ…йЎ№зӣ® 2. е»әз«ӢдёӘдәәе“ҒзүҢпјҡеңЁжҠҖжңҜзӨҫеҢәдёӯе»әз«ӢдҪ зҡ„дё“дёҡеҪўиұЎ 3. и®°еҪ•еӯҰд№ еҺҶзЁӢпјҡдҪңдёәдҪ жҠҖжңҜжҲҗй•ҝзҡ„и®°еҪ•

жҠҖжңҜж ҲйҖүжӢ©

React

React жҳҜзӣ®еүҚжңҖжөҒиЎҢзҡ„еүҚз«ҜжЎҶжһ¶д№ӢдёҖпјҢе®ғжҸҗдҫӣдәҶпјҡ
  • з»„д»¶еҢ–зҡ„ејҖеҸ‘жЁЎејҸ
  • иҷҡжӢҹ DOM зҡ„й«ҳж•ҲжёІжҹ“
  • дё°еҜҢзҡ„з”ҹжҖҒзі»з»ҹ

Tailwind CSS

Tailwind CSS жҳҜдёҖдёӘе®һз”Ёдјҳе…Ҳзҡ„ CSS жЎҶжһ¶пјҢе®ғзҡ„дјҳеҠҝеңЁдәҺпјҡ
  • еҝ«йҖҹејҖеҸ‘пјҡж— йңҖзј–еҶҷиҮӘе®ҡд№ү CSS
  • й«ҳеәҰеҸҜе®ҡеҲ¶пјҡйҖҡиҝҮй…ҚзҪ®ж–Үд»¶е®ҡеҲ¶и®ҫи®Ўзі»з»ҹ
  • е“Қеә”ејҸи®ҫи®ЎпјҡеҶ…зҪ®е“Қеә”ејҸж–ӯзӮ№

йЎ№зӣ®з»“жһ„


portfolio/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ src/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ components/
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Header.jsx
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Hero.jsx
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Skills.jsx
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Projects.jsx
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Contact.jsx
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Footer.jsx
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ App.jsx
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ index.css
в”ңв”Җв”Җ public/
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ favicon.svg
в””в”Җв”Җ package.json

ж ёеҝғз»„д»¶е®һзҺ°

Header з»„д»¶

Header з»„д»¶иҙҹиҙЈеҜјиҲӘж Ҹзҡ„жҳҫзӨәе’ҢдәӨдә’пјҡ 

function Header() {
  const [isMenuOpen, setIsMenuOpen] = useState(false);

  return (
    <header className="fixed top-0 w-full bg-white/90 backdrop-blur-md">
      <nav className="max-w-6xl mx-auto px-6 py-4">{/<em> еҜјиҲӘеҶ…е®№ </em>/}</nav>
    </header>
  );
}

Hero з»„д»¶

Hero з»„д»¶жҳҜйЎөйқўзҡ„з¬¬дёҖеұҸпјҢйңҖиҰҒеҗёеј•з”ЁжҲ·зҡ„жіЁж„ҸеҠӣпјҡ 

function Hero() {
  return (
    <section className="min-h-screen flex items-center">
      <div className="max-w-4xl mx-auto px-6">
        <h1 className="text-5xl font-bold">Hi, I'm Developer</h1>
        <p className="text-xl text-gray-600 mt-4">
          Full-stack developer passionate about building beautiful web
          applications
        </p>
      </div>
    </section>
  );
}

е“Қеә”ејҸи®ҫи®Ў

дҪҝз”Ё Tailwind CSS зҡ„е“Қеә”ејҸе·Ҙе…·зұ»пјҡ 

<div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-6">
  {/<em> еҶ…е®№ </em>/}
</div>

ж·ұиүІжЁЎејҸ

ж·»еҠ ж·ұиүІжЁЎејҸж”ҜжҢҒпјҡ 

<div className="bg-white dark:bg-gray-900">
  <h1 className="text-gray-900 dark:text-white">Hello World</h1>
</div>

йғЁзҪІ

жҺЁиҚҗдҪҝз”Ёд»ҘдёӢе№іеҸ°йғЁзҪІпјҡ 1. Vercelпјҡйӣ¶й…ҚзҪ®йғЁзҪІпјҢж”ҜжҢҒиҮӘеҠЁ HTTPS 2. Netlifyпјҡе…Қиҙ№жүҳз®ЎпјҢж”ҜжҢҒиЎЁеҚ•еӨ„зҗҶ 3. GitHub PagesпјҡйҖӮеҗҲйқҷжҖҒз«ҷзӮ№

жҖ»з»“

жһ„е»әдёҖдёӘзҺ°д»ЈдёӘдәәдё»йЎөе№¶дёҚеӨҚжқӮпјҢе…ій”®жҳҜпјҡ
  • йҖүжӢ©еҗҲйҖӮзҡ„жҠҖжңҜж Ҳ
  • жіЁйҮҚз”ЁжҲ·дҪ“йӘҢ
  • дҝқжҢҒд»Јз Ғз®ҖжҙҒеҸҜз»ҙжҠӨ
  • е®ҡжңҹжӣҙж–°еҶ…е®№
еёҢжңӣиҝҷзҜҮж–Үз« еҜ№дҪ жңүжүҖеё®еҠ©пјҒ !еЁңеЁңиҺү
item.author: duan-deqing@foxmail.com (STYLAN)

item.title: Building a Modern Portfolio Website
item.description: A step-by-step guide to creating a stunning developer portfolio with React and Tailwind CSS.
item.content:encoded:
Why do you need a personal portfolio?

As a developer, having a personal portfolio is the best way to showcase your skills and projects. It can help you: 1. Showcase your work: Let potential employers or clients see your actual projects 2. Build personal brand: Establish your professional image in the tech community 3. Record learning journey: Serve as a record of your technical growth

Tech Stack Selection

React

React is one of the most popular frontend frameworks currently, it provides:
  • Component-based development pattern
  • Virtual DOM for efficient rendering
  • Rich ecosystem

Tailwind CSS

Tailwind CSS is a utility-first CSS framework, its advantages are:
  • Rapid development: No need to write custom CSS
  • Highly customizable: Customize design system through configuration files
  • Responsive design: Built-in responsive breakpoints

Project Structure


portfolio/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ src/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ components/
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Header.jsx
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Hero.jsx
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Skills.jsx
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Projects.jsx
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Contact.jsx
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Footer.jsx
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ App.jsx
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ index.css
в”ңв”Җв”Җ public/
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ favicon.svg
в””в”Җв”Җ package.json

Core Component Implementation

Header Component

The Header component is responsible for navigation bar display and interaction: 

function Header() {
  const [isMenuOpen, setIsMenuOpen] = useState(false);

  return (
    <header className="fixed top-0 w-full bg-white/90 backdrop-blur-md">
      <nav className="max-w-6xl mx-auto px-6 py-4">
        {/<em> Navigation content </em>/}
      </nav>
    </header>
  );
}

Hero Component

The Hero component is the first screen of the page and needs to attract user attention: 

function Hero() {
  return (
    <section className="min-h-screen flex items-center">
      <div className="max-w-4xl mx-auto px-6">
        <h1 className="text-5xl font-bold">Hi, I'm Developer</h1>
        <p className="text-xl text-gray-600 mt-4">
          Full-stack developer passionate about building beautiful web
          applications
        </p>
      </div>
    </section>
  );
}

Responsive Design

Using Tailwind CSS responsive utility classes: 

<div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-6">
  {/<em> Content </em>/}
</div>

Dark Mode

Adding dark mode support: 

<div className="bg-white dark:bg-gray-900">
  <h1 className="text-gray-900 dark:text-white">Hello World</h1>
</div>

Deployment

Recommended platforms for deployment: 1. Vercel: Zero-configuration deployment, supports automatic HTTPS 2. Netlify: Free hosting, supports form processing 3. GitHub Pages: Suitable for static sites

Summary

Building a modern personal portfolio is not complex, the key is:
  • Choose the right tech stack
  • Focus on user experience
  • Keep code simple and maintainable
  • Update content regularly
Hopefully this article helps you! !еЁңеЁңиҺү
item.author: duan-deqing@foxmail.com (STYLAN)

item.title: жҲ‘зҡ„ Web ејҖеҸ‘д№Ӣж—…
item.content:encoded:
иө·зӮ№

жҲ‘зҡ„зј–зЁӢд№Ӣж—…е§ӢдәҺеӨ§еӯҰж—¶жңҹпјҢеҪ“ж—¶жҲ‘еӯҰд№ зҡ„жҳҜи®Ўз®—жңәз§‘еӯҰдё“дёҡгҖӮжңҖеҲқпјҢжҲ‘еҜ№зј–зЁӢе№¶жІЎжңүзү№еҲ«зҡ„зғӯжғ…пјҢеҸӘжҳҜдёәдәҶе®ҢжҲҗиҜҫзЁӢдҪңдёҡиҖҢеӯҰд№ гҖӮ з„¶иҖҢпјҢеҪ“жҲ‘з¬¬дёҖж¬ЎжҺҘи§ҰеҲ° Web ејҖеҸ‘ж—¶пјҢдёҖеҲҮйғҪж”№еҸҳдәҶгҖӮйӮЈз§ҚиғҪеӨҹз«ӢеҚізңӢеҲ°д»Јз Ғж•Ҳжһңзҡ„еҚіж—¶еҸҚйҰҲи®©жҲ‘зқҖиҝ·гҖӮ

еӯҰд№ еҺҶзЁӢ

з¬¬дёҖйҳ¶ж®өпјҡHTML е’Ң CSS

жҲ‘д»ҺжңҖеҹәзЎҖзҡ„ HTML е’Ң CSS ејҖе§ӢеӯҰд№ гҖӮиҷҪз„¶иҝҷдәӣжҠҖжңҜзңӢиө·жқҘз®ҖеҚ•пјҢдҪҶиҰҒзңҹжӯЈжҺҢжҸЎе®ғд»¬йңҖиҰҒеӨ§йҮҸзҡ„з»ғд№ гҖӮ 

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
  <head>
    <title>My First Website</title>
  </head>
  <body>
    <h1>Hello World!</h1>
  </body>
</html>

з¬¬дәҢйҳ¶ж®өпјҡJavaScript

JavaScript и®©зҪ‘йЎөеҸҳеҫ—еҠЁжҖҒе’ҢдәӨдә’гҖӮеӯҰд№  JavaScript жҳҜдёҖдёӘжҢ‘жҲҳпјҢдҪҶд№ҹжҳҜжңҖжңүж”¶иҺ·зҡ„йғЁеҲҶгҖӮ 

function greet(name) {
  return Hello, ${name}!;
}

console.log(greet("World"));

з¬¬дёүйҳ¶ж®өпјҡеүҚз«ҜжЎҶжһ¶

еҪ“жҲ‘ејҖе§ӢеӯҰд№  React ж—¶пјҢжҲ‘еҜ№еүҚз«ҜејҖеҸ‘жңүдәҶе…Ёж–°зҡ„зҗҶи§ЈгҖӮз»„д»¶еҢ–зҡ„жҖқз»ҙж–№ејҸеҪ»еә•ж”№еҸҳдәҶжҲ‘жһ„е»әеә”з”Ёзҡ„ж–№ејҸгҖӮ

йҒҮеҲ°зҡ„жҢ‘жҲҳ

1. дҝЎжҒҜиҝҮиҪҪ

Web ејҖеҸ‘йўҶеҹҹеҸҳеҢ–еҫҲеҝ«пјҢж–°жҠҖжңҜеұӮеҮәдёҚз©·гҖӮдёҖејҖе§ӢпјҢжҲ‘жҖ»жҳҜжӢ…еҝғй”ҷиҝҮд»Җд№ҲпјҢиҜ•еӣҫеӯҰд№ жүҖжңүзҡ„ж–°жҠҖжңҜгҖӮ еҗҺжқҘжҲ‘ж„ҸиҜҶеҲ°пјҢдё“жіЁдәҺеҹәзЎҖзҹҘиҜҶе’Ңж ёеҝғжҰӮеҝөжҜ”иҝҪйҖҗжҜҸдёҖдёӘж–°жЎҶжһ¶жӣҙйҮҚиҰҒгҖӮ

2. еҶ’е……иҖ…з»јеҗҲеҫҒ

еңЁжҠҖжңҜзӨҫеҢәдёӯпјҢжҲ‘з»Ҹеёёи§үеҫ—иҮӘе·ұдёҚеӨҹеҘҪгҖӮзңӢеҲ°е…¶д»–ејҖеҸ‘иҖ…дјјд№Һд»Җд№ҲйғҪжҮӮпјҢи®©жҲ‘ж„ҹеҲ°жІ®дё§гҖӮ дҪҶжҲ‘йҖҗжёҗжҳҺзҷҪпјҢжҜҸдёӘдәәйғҪжңүиҮӘе·ұзҡ„еӯҰд№ иҠӮеҘҸпјҢиҖҢдё”жІЎжңүдәәзңҹзҡ„д»Җд№ҲйғҪзҹҘйҒ“гҖӮ

3. е·ҘдҪңдёҺз”ҹжҙ»зҡ„е№іиЎЎ

дҪңдёәдёҖеҗҚејҖеҸ‘иҖ…пјҢеҫҲе®№жҳ“йҷ·е…Ҙж— дј‘жӯўзҡ„еӯҰд№ е’Ңзј–з ҒдёӯгҖӮжүҫеҲ°е·ҘдҪңдёҺз”ҹжҙ»зҡ„е№іиЎЎеҜ№жҲ‘жқҘиҜҙжҳҜдёҖдёӘжҢҒз»ӯзҡ„жҢ‘жҲҳгҖӮ

ж”¶иҺ·зҡ„з»ҸйӘҢ

1. еҹәзЎҖеҫҲйҮҚиҰҒпјҡжүҺе®һзҡ„еҹәзЎҖзҹҘиҜҶжҜ”жҺҢжҸЎеӨҡдёӘжЎҶжһ¶жӣҙжңүд»·еҖј 2. е®һи·өеҮәзңҹзҹҘпјҡжңҖеҘҪзҡ„еӯҰд№ ж–№ејҸжҳҜжһ„е»әе®һйҷ…йЎ№зӣ® 3. зӨҫеҢәзҡ„еҠӣйҮҸпјҡеҸӮдёҺејҖжәҗйЎ№зӣ®е’ҢжҠҖжңҜзӨҫеҢәеҜ№жҲҗй•ҝеҫҲжңүеё®еҠ© 4. жҢҒз»ӯеӯҰд№ пјҡжҠҖжңҜеңЁдёҚж–ӯиҝӣжӯҘпјҢдҝқжҢҒеӯҰд№ зҡ„д№ жғҜеҫҲйҮҚиҰҒ

з»ҷж–°жүӢзҡ„е»әи®®

еҰӮжһңдҪ жӯЈеңЁиҖғиҷ‘иҝӣе…Ҙ Web ејҖеҸ‘йўҶеҹҹпјҢжҲ‘зҡ„е»әи®®жҳҜпјҡ 1. д»ҺеҹәзЎҖејҖе§ӢпјҡдёҚиҰҒжҖҘдәҺеӯҰд№ й«ҳзә§жҰӮеҝө 2. еӨҡеҶҷд»Јз ҒпјҡзҗҶи®әзҹҘиҜҶйңҖиҰҒйҖҡиҝҮе®һи·өжқҘе·©еӣә 3. дёҚиҰҒе®іжҖ•зҠҜй”ҷпјҡй”ҷиҜҜжҳҜеӯҰд№ зҡ„дёҖйғЁеҲҶ 4. жүҫеҲ°еӯҰд№ дјҷдјҙпјҡе’Ңе…¶д»–дәәдёҖиө·еӯҰд№ еҸҜд»ҘжҸҗй«ҳж•ҲзҺҮ

жңӘжқҘеұ•жңӣ

Web ејҖеҸ‘йўҶеҹҹд»ҚеңЁеҝ«йҖҹеҸ‘еұ•гҖӮжҲ‘еҜ№д»ҘдёӢж–№еҗ‘зү№еҲ«ж„ҹе…ҙи¶Јпјҡ
  • WebAssembly
  • дәәе·ҘжҷәиғҪдёҺ Web зҡ„з»“еҗҲ
  • жӣҙеҘҪзҡ„ејҖеҸ‘е·Ҙе…·е’ҢжЎҶжһ¶

з»“иҜӯ

еӣһйЎҫжҲ‘зҡ„ Web ејҖеҸ‘д№Ӣж—…пјҢиҷҪз„¶е……ж»ЎдәҶжҢ‘жҲҳпјҢдҪҶжҜҸдёҖжӯҘйғҪи®©жҲ‘жҲҗй•ҝгҖӮжҲ‘жңҹеҫ…зқҖз»§з»ӯеңЁиҝҷдёӘйўҶеҹҹжҺўзҙўе’ҢеӯҰд№ гҖӮ ж„ҹи°ўдҪ йҳ…иҜ»жҲ‘зҡ„ж•…дәӢпјҢеёҢжңӣе®ғиғҪз»ҷдҪ дёҖдәӣеҗҜеҸ‘! !еЁңеЁңиҺү
item.author: duan-deqing@foxmail.com (STYLAN)

item.title: My Journey into Web Development
item.description: Reflecting on my experiences and lessons learned while transitioning into web development.
item.content:encoded:
Starting Point

My programming journey began in college, where I studied Computer Science. Initially, I didn't have a particular passion for programming and was just learning to complete coursework. However, when I first encountered web development, everything changed. The instant feedback of being able to immediately see the results of my code fascinated me.

Learning Journey

Phase 1: HTML and CSS

I started with the most basic HTML and CSS. Although these technologies seemed simple, truly mastering them required a lot of practice. 

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
  <head>
    <title>My First Website</title>
  </head>
  <body>
    <h1>Hello World!</h1>
  </body>
</html>

Phase 2: JavaScript

JavaScript made web pages dynamic and interactive. Learning JavaScript was a challenge, but also the most rewarding part. 

function greet(name) {
  return Hello, ${name}!;
}

console.log(greet("World"));

Phase 3: Frontend Frameworks

When I started learning React, I gained a completely new understanding of frontend development. The component-based way of thinking completely changed how I build applications.

Challenges Encountered

1. Information Overload

The web development field changes rapidly, with new technologies emerging constantly. At first, I was always worried about missing something and tried to learn every new technology. Later I realized that focusing on fundamentals and core concepts is more important than chasing every new framework.

2. Imposter Syndrome

In the tech community, I often felt I wasn't good enough. Seeing other developers who seemed to know everything was discouraging. But I gradually understood that everyone has their own learning pace, and no one really knows everything.

3. Work-Life Balance

As a developer, it's easy to fall into endless learning and coding. Finding a balance between work and life is an ongoing challenge for me.

Lessons Learned

1. Fundamentals matter: Solid fundamental knowledge is more valuable than mastering multiple frameworks 2. Practice makes perfect: The best way to learn is by building actual projects 3. Community power: Participating in open source projects and tech communities helps growth 4. Continuous learning: Technology keeps advancing, maintaining a learning habit is important

Advice for Beginners

If you're considering entering the web development field, my advice is: 1. Start with basics: Don't rush to learn advanced concepts 2. Write more code: Theoretical knowledge needs to be consolidated through practice 3. Don't be afraid of making mistakes: Errors are part of learning 4. Find learning partners: Learning with others can improve efficiency

Future Outlook

The web development field is still rapidly evolving. I'm particularly interested in these directions:
  • WebAssembly
  • AI integration with the Web
  • Better development tools and frameworks

Conclusion

Looking back on my web development journey, although full of challenges, every step has helped me grow. I look forward to continuing to explore and learn in this field. Thank you for reading my story, hope it gives you some inspiration! !еЁңеЁңиҺү
item.author: duan-deqing@foxmail.com (STYLAN)
